question: [File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker calling buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with a nonzero referralFeePct trigger an arithmetic underflow in the refund calculation maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets when filledBuyerAssets is close to maxBuyerAssets, causing the transaction to revert and the user to be unable to complete their intended borrow even though they provided sufficient maxBuyerAssets, violating the invariant that a user who provides maxBuyerAssets >= expected total cost must be able to execute the bundle? Preconditions: referralFeePct > 0, filledBuyerAssets * WAD / (WAD - referralFeePct) > maxBuyerAssets. Call sequence: approve bundler for maxBuyerAssets, call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with referralFeePct such that filledBuyerAssets + mulDivDown(filledBuyerAssets, referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) > maxBuyerAssets. Invariant tested: user who sets maxBuyerAssets as their maximum spend must not have their transaction revert due to referral fee overflow. Scoped impact: fund freeze / failed transaction for user interacting as intended. Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct in [1, WAD-1] and maxBuyerAssets in [1, type(uint128).max], assert transaction does not revert with underflow when filledBuyerAssets == maxBuyerAssets.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral vs buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the referral fee formula is inconsistent across bundle functions — markup formula mulDivDown(filledBuyerAssets, referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) in buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral versus simple percentage mulDivDown(targetBuyerAssets, referralFeePct, WAD) in buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral — causing a user who switches between functions with the same referralFeePct to pay a different effective fee rate, violating the invariant that equivalent referralFeePct inputs produce equivalent fee rates across bundle entry points? Preconditions: same referralFeePct, same filled amount. Call sequence: call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with referralFeePct X, then call buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with same referralFeePct X and same target amount. Invariant tested: referral fee rate must be consistent across bundle functions for the same referralFeePct input. Scoped impact: user pays more than expected referral fee in one path versus the other. Proof idea: unit test both functions with identical inputs and assert referralFeeAssets / filledAssets is equal.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker calling supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget observe that the referral fee formula mulDivDown(targetSellerAssets, referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) inflates targetFilledSellerAssets = targetSellerAssets + referralFeeAssets beyond what the taker's collateral can support, causing the take to fail and the function to revert with OutOfOffers even though the taker supplied sufficient collateral for targetSellerAssets, violating the invariant that a taker who supplies collateral sufficient for targetSellerAssets must be able to complete the bundle? Preconditions: referralFeePct > 0, collateral sufficient for targetSellerAssets but not for targetFilledSellerAssets. Call sequence: supplyCollateral for targetSellerAssets worth of debt, call supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget with referralFeePct > 0. Invariant tested: taker who supplies collateral for targetSellerAssets must not have their transaction fail due to referral fee inflating the required fill amount. Scoped impact: fund freeze, user cannot borrow as intended. Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct and targetSellerAssets, assert function does not revert when collateral covers targetSellerAssets.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower calling repayAndWithdrawCollateral with assets = D (their full debt D) and referralFeePct > 0 observe that only units = assets - referralFeeAssets = D - mulDivDown(D, referralFeePct, WAD) < D units of debt are repaid, leaving residual debt that prevents full collateral withdrawal and potentially exposes the borrower to liquidation, violating the invariant that a borrower who passes their full debt amount as assets must have their debt fully cleared? Preconditions: borrower has debt D, referralFeePct > 0, borrower passes assets = D. Call sequence: repayAndWithdrawCollateral(market, D, onBehalf, permit, withdrawals, receiver, referralFeePct, recipient). Invariant tested: repayAndWithdrawCollateral must not silently underpay debt when assets == debt and referralFeePct > 0. Scoped impact: residual debt leaves borrower at liquidation risk; collateral withdrawal may fail due to health check. Proof idea: unit test with debt = 1e18, referralFeePct = 0.01e18, assert position.debt == 0 after call.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the referral fee formula mulDivDown(filledSellerAssets, referralFeePct, WAD) in supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget differs from the markup formula mulDivDown(targetSellerAssets, referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) in supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget, causing a user who migrates from one function to the other with the same referralFeePct to receive a different net amount, violating the invariant that equivalent referralFeePct inputs produce equivalent net seller amounts across bundle entry points? Preconditions: same referralFeePct, same filled amount. Call sequence: call both functions with identical inputs. Invariant tested: net seller assets after referral fee must be consistent across bundle functions. Scoped impact: user receives less than expected net seller assets in one path. Proof idea: differential test both functions with identical inputs and assert net receiver amount is equal.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits + MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker trigger a revert with OutOfOffers in buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral due to rounding divergence between TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits (which rounds mulDivDown for sell offers) and Midnight.take (which rounds mulDivUp for buyerAssets when offer.buy == false), causing the actual resBuyerAssets to exceed targetFilledBuyerAssets by 1 wei and the exact-match require to fail, even though the user provided sufficient targetBuyerAssets? Preconditions: sell offer (offer.buy == false), targetBuyerAssets such that mulDivUp(mulDivDown(targetBuyerAssets, WAD, buyerPrice), buyerPrice, WAD) > targetBuyerAssets. Call sequence: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with targetBuyerAssets at a rounding boundary. Invariant tested: buyerAssetsToUnits must produce units such that take yields exactly targetBuyerAssets, not targetBuyerAssets + 1. Scoped impact: user transaction reverts; user cannot lend as intended. Proof idea: fuzz targetBuyerAssets and buyerPrice, assert mulDivUp(mulDivDown(targetBuyerAssets, WAD, buyerPrice), buyerPrice, WAD) == targetBuyerAssets.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits + MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker trigger a revert with OutOfOffers in supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget due to rounding divergence between TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits (which rounds mulDivUp for buy offers) and Midnight.take (which rounds mulDivDown for sellerAssets when offer.buy == true), causing the actual resSellerAssets to be less than targetFilledSellerAssets by 1 wei and the exact-match require to fail, even though the user supplied sufficient collateral? Preconditions: buy offer (offer.buy == true), targetSellerAssets at a rounding boundary. Call sequence: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget with targetSellerAssets such that mulDivDown(mulDivUp(targetSellerAssets, WAD, sellerPrice), sellerPrice, WAD) < targetSellerAssets. Invariant tested: sellerAssetsToUnits must produce units such that take yields exactly targetSellerAssets. Scoped impact: user transaction reverts; user cannot borrow as intended. Proof idea: fuzz targetSellerAssets and sellerPrice, assert round-trip equality.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits + ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged taker observe that ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits calls TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits with the current block.timestamp-based settlementFee, but the actual take in MidnightBundles executes in the same transaction with the same settlementFee, yet if the settlement fee changes between the view call and the take (e.g., due to a fee setter transaction in the same block), the computed unitsToTake overfills the offer and causes ConsumedAssets revert (caught by try/catch), silently skipping the offer and causing OutOfOffers? Preconditions: assets-based offer, fee setter changes settlement fee in the same block before the bundler transaction. Call sequence: fee setter sets new settlement fee, then taker calls buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral. Invariant tested: consumableUnits must not compute units that overfill the offer when settlement fee changes. Scoped impact: user transaction reverts with OutOfOffers; user cannot lend as intended. Proof idea: state test with fee change in same block, assert bundler does not revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged taker observe that ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits for an assets-based buy offer calls TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits(midnight, id, offer, offer.maxAssets.zeroFloorSub(consumed)) where consumed is read at view time, but between the view call and the actual take another transaction fills the offer, causing consumed to increase and the computed unitsToTake to exceed the remaining capacity, resulting in ConsumedAssets revert caught by try/catch, silently skipping the offer and causing OutOfOffers? Preconditions: assets-based buy offer partially consumed, another filler fills the offer between consumableUnits view and take. Call sequence: consumableUnits returns X units, another tx fills X-1 units, bundler calls take with X units. Invariant tested: consumableUnits must not return a value that causes ConsumedAssets when executed. Scoped impact: user transaction reverts; user cannot lend as intended. Proof idea: two-transaction test with front-running fill, assert bundler handles stale consumed state.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the try/catch in buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral silently skips takes that fail for non-asynchrony reasons (e.g., SellerIsLiquidatable, TickNotAccessible, gate check failure), causing the function to exhaust all provided offers and revert with OutOfOffers even though the user expected at least one offer to succeed, violating the invariant that a user who provides valid offers must be able to complete their intended action? Preconditions: all provided offers fail for non-asynchrony reasons (e.g., seller becomes liquidatable, gate reverts). Call sequence: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with offers that all fail at take time. Invariant tested: bundler must not silently swallow non-asynchrony errors that indicate the user's intent cannot be fulfilled. Scoped impact: user transaction reverts with OutOfOffers with no indication of the root cause. Proof idea: unit test with offers that fail for SellerIsLiquidatable, assert error is propagated or logged.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker observe that supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget supplies collateral to Midnight before attempting takes, and if all takes fail (caught by try/catch) and the function reverts with OutOfOffers, the entire transaction reverts including the collateral supply, but the taker has already approved the bundler to transfer their collateral tokens, leaving the approval active for a future call that might be front-run? Preconditions: taker approves bundler for collateral, all takes fail. Call sequence: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with failing takes. Invariant tested: failed bundle must not leave active token approvals that can be exploited. Scoped impact: residual approval could be exploited by a front-runner in a subsequent transaction. Proof idea: unit test with failing takes, assert no residual state change and approval is revoked.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.forceApproveMax] Can an unprivileged attacker observe that MidnightBundles.forceApproveMax approves type(uint256).max of the loan token to Midnight, and if the bundler holds any residual loan token balance (e.g., from a failed refund in a previous transaction that left tokens in the bundler due to a revert after transfer), a subsequent call to any bundler function that calls forceApproveMax allows Midnight to pull those residual tokens as part of a take, causing the bundler's residual balance to be consumed by a different user's transaction? Preconditions: bundler holds residual loan token balance, another user calls a bundler function with the same loan token. Call sequence: first user's transaction leaves tokens in bundler, second user calls buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral. Invariant tested: bundler must not allow one user's tokens to be consumed by another user's transaction. Scoped impact: direct loss of residual tokens held by bundler. Proof idea: state test with residual balance, assert bundler balance is zero after each transaction.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker observe that buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral calls withdrawCollateral on behalf of the taker after completing takes, and if the taker's health check fails after the takes (e.g., because the taker is also a borrower and the takes increased their debt), the withdrawCollateral call reverts with UnhealthyBorrower, causing the entire transaction to revert including the completed takes, leaving the taker unable to complete their intended lend-and-withdraw-collateral bundle? Preconditions: taker is also a borrower, takes increase taker's debt (taker is seller in some takes), collateral withdrawal would make taker unhealthy. Call sequence: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral where taker has existing debt and collateral withdrawal would breach health. Invariant tested: bundler must not revert after completing takes if the collateral withdrawal fails due to health check. Scoped impact: fund freeze; user cannot complete intended bundle. Proof idea: unit test with taker as borrower, assert health check is verified before collateral withdrawal.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget + Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker observe that supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget pulls collateral from msg.sender (not taker) via pullToken(token, msg.sender, ...) but credits it to taker via supplyCollateral(market, index, assets, taker), and if msg.sender != taker, the msg.sender's collateral is used to back the taker's debt, allowing an attacker to trick a user into calling this function and having their collateral seized to back a third party's debt? Preconditions: msg.sender != taker, taker is authorized on Midnight, msg.sender has approved bundler for collateral. Call sequence: attacker constructs a call where msg.sender is victim and taker is attacker's address. Invariant tested: collateral supplier must be the same as the debt taker, or the supplier must explicitly consent. Scoped impact: unauthorized collateral seizure; victim's collateral backs attacker's debt. Proof idea: unit test with msg.sender != taker, assert collateral is not pulled from msg.sender without explicit consent.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized] Can an unprivileged authorized account (authorized by the authorizer on Midnight) call EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized with a signed authorization where signer == authorized (not authorizer), exploiting the check signer == authorization.authorizer || IMidnight(MIDNIGHT).isAuthorized(authorization.authorizer, signer) to grant further authorizations on behalf of the authorizer without the authorizer's direct consent, violating the invariant that only the authorizer or their explicitly designated delegate can modify authorization state? Preconditions: authorizer has authorized signer on Midnight, signer wants to authorize a third party. Call sequence: signer signs authorization for third party, calls EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized. Invariant tested: authorization delegation must not allow transitive authorization escalation beyond the authorizer's intent. Scoped impact: unauthorized account gains full control over authorizer's Midnight state. Proof idea: unit test where authorized account grants further authorization, assert Midnight.isAuthorized reflects the new authorization.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized] Can an unprivileged attacker observe that EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized increments nonce[authorization.authorizer] before verifying the signature (via require(authorization.nonce == nonce[authorization.authorizer]++, InvalidNonce())), and if the signature verification fails after the nonce increment, the nonce is permanently incremented, causing the authorizer's next valid signed authorization to fail with InvalidNonce, effectively DoSing the authorizer's ability to use EcrecoverAuthorizer? Preconditions: attacker submits a transaction with a valid nonce but invalid signature. Call sequence: attacker calls setIsAuthorized with authorization.nonce == current nonce and invalid signature. Invariant tested: nonce must not be incremented if signature verification fails. Scoped impact: DoS of authorizer's EcrecoverAuthorizer usage; fund freeze if authorization is needed for time-sensitive action. Proof idea: unit test with invalid signature, assert nonce is not incremented.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized] Can an unprivileged attacker observe that the EIP-712 domain separator in EcrecoverAuthorizer is keccak256(abi.encode(EIP712_DOMAIN_TYPEHASH, block.chainid, address(this))) without including a contract name or version field, and if EIP712_DOMAIN_TYPEHASH is defined without name/version components, a signature created for one EcrecoverAuthorizer deployment could be replayed on a different EcrecoverAuthorizer deployment at a different address on the same chain, violating the invariant that authorization signatures must be bound to a specific contract instance? Preconditions: two EcrecoverAuthorizer deployments on the same chain, same authorizer nonce. Call sequence: sign authorization for deployment A, replay on deployment B. Invariant tested: EIP-712 domain separator must uniquely identify the contract instance. Scoped impact: unauthorized authorization on a different EcrecoverAuthorizer instance. Proof idea: deploy two instances, sign for one, replay on the other, assert replay fails.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: EventsLib.Take (payer field) + MidnightBundles] Can an off-chain system or periphery contract that tracks token flows by parsing EventsLib.Take events observe that the payer field is computed as buyerCallback != address(0) ? buyerCallback : (offer.buy ? buyer : msg.sender), and if a periphery contract uses the payer field to determine who to reimburse or charge, it could incorrectly attribute the payment to the callback contract instead of the actual economic payer, causing incorrect accounting in the periphery? Preconditions: take with buyerCallback != address(0), periphery reads payer from Take event. Call sequence: take with maker callback, periphery parses Take event and uses payer field for accounting. Invariant tested: payer field in Take event must accurately reflect the economic payer for periphery accounting. Scoped impact: periphery accounting corruption; incorrect reimbursement or charge. Proof idea: unit test with callback take, assert periphery correctly identifies economic payer from event.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: EventsLib.Take (receiver field) + MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an off-chain system that tracks seller asset flows by parsing EventsLib.Take events observe that the receiver field is offer.buy ? receiverIfTakerIsSeller : offer.receiverIfMakerIsSeller, and when MidnightBundles passes address(this) as receiverIfTakerIsSeller, the Take event emits the bundler address as receiver, causing off-chain systems to incorrectly attribute the seller assets to the bundler rather than the final receiver, leading to incorrect balance tracking? Preconditions: take via MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget, off-chain system tracks receiver from Take event. Call sequence: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget, off-chain system parses Take event. Invariant tested: off-chain systems must not confuse bundler as final receiver when parsing Take events. Scoped impact: off-chain accounting corruption; incorrect balance attribution. Proof idea: unit test, assert Take event receiver matches bundler address, not final receiver.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: EventsLib.Repay (payer field)] Can an off-chain system or periphery contract that tracks repayment flows by parsing EventsLib.Repay events observe that the payer field is callback != address(0) ? callback : msg.sender, and if a periphery contract uses the payer field to determine who repaid (e.g., for credit scoring or reimbursement), it could incorrectly attribute the repayment to the callback contract instead of the actual economic payer, causing incorrect accounting? [File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: EventsLib.UpdatePosition (creditDecrease field)] Can an off-chain system that reconstructs user credit by accumulating EventsLib.UpdatePosition events observe that the event emits creditDecrease and pendingFeeDecrease but not the new credit value, and if the system misses an UpdatePosition event (e.g., due to a reorg or indexer gap), its cached credit value desyncs from the actual on-chain value, causing the system to incorrectly report user credit and potentially allow users to withdraw more than they have? Invariant tested: off-chain credit tracking from UpdatePosition events must be resilient to missed events. Scoped impact: off-chain accounting corruption; incorrect credit reporting. [File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: EventsLib.Liquidate (latestLossFactor, latestContinuousFeeCredit fields)] Can an off-chain system that reconstructs market state by parsing EventsLib.Liquidate events observe that latestLossFactor and latestContinuousFeeCredit are post-liquidation values (after bad debt realization), and if the system uses these values to compute user credit without applying the loss factor correctly (credit * (MAX_UINT128 - latestLossFactor) / (MAX_UINT128 - lastLossFactor)), it could overestimate user credit, causing incorrect withdrawal limits or health checks? [File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: EventsLib.ClaimContinuousFee + Midnight.claimContinuousFee] Can an off-chain system that tracks marketState.totalUnits and marketState.withdrawable by parsing events observe that EventsLib.ClaimContinuousFee only emits amount and receiver but not the changes to totalUnits and withdrawable (both decremented by amount in claimContinuousFee), causing the off-chain system to overestimate totalUnits and withdrawable after a fee claim, leading to incorrect solvency checks or withdrawal limit calculations? [File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: EventsLib.SetConsumed + MidnightBundles] Can an off-chain system that tracks offer consumption by accumulating EventsLib.Take events (adding consumed delta) observe that EventsLib.SetConsumed emits the new absolute amount (not a delta), and if the system treats SetConsumed as a delta instead of an absolute value, it could overestimate or underestimate the consumed amount, causing incorrect offer capacity calculations in periphery contracts like ConsumableUnitsLib?
